steps.title: Find the gesture
steps.description: Lightly sketch a single curving line from the base of the trunk up through where the highest branch will reach. This is the tree's overall posture — most trees lean or curve slightly.

steps.title: Draw the trunk
steps.description: Build the trunk along your gesture line with two slightly curving vertical lines. Make it wider at the base, narrower toward the top — trees taper as they rise.

steps.title: Sketch the main branches
steps.description: Add 3–5 main branches coming off the trunk at varying angles. Branches taper too — narrower at their tips than where they join the trunk.

steps.title: Add smaller branches
steps.description: Add smaller branches splitting off the main ones. Each split should make the branches slightly thinner. Don't make every branch the same length.

steps.title: Outline the foliage
steps.description: Draw cloud-like shapes around the branch tips for clusters of leaves. Use a slightly bumpy line, not a smooth curve. Leave some gaps so a few branches peek through.

steps.title: Vary line weight
steps.description: Go back over the trunk and the undersides of branches with slightly heavier lines. This thickening at shadow areas adds depth even in pure line art.

steps.title: Suggest texture
steps.description: Add a few short curved lines on the trunk for bark texture and a small handful of leaf shapes inside the foliage clusters. Suggest, don't render — a few marks imply many.
